private_memo:
# Morning memo — 2026-06-27

**Source failures:** None observed.

## Worth attention

- **AI SDK 7 is now available** — https://vercel.com/changelog/ai-sdk-7
  Vercel released AI SDK 7, a major TypeScript AI/agent SDK update (16M weekly downloads). Breaking changes require migration. Key additions: agent harness adapters to run Claude Code, Codex, OpenCode, and Deep Agents behind a unified interface; standardized reasoning control across providers; typed tool context for 3rd-party tools; file/skill upload APIs to avoid re-sending large blobs per call; MCP Apps support for mixing model-visible and app-only tools. Run the v7 codemods before your next deploy.

- **What happened after 2k people tried to hack my AI assistant** — https://www.fernandoi.cl/posts/hackmyclaw/
  A builder exposed their AI legal assistant to 2,000+ people with an open invitation to break it. Real-world findings on prompt injection, jailbreaking, and boundary testing in production AI apps. If you ship any AI tool with user-controlled input, this is directly relevant security intelligence.

- **Teaching agents product design at Vercel** — https://vercel.com/blog/teaching-agents-product-design-at-vercel
  Vercel describes how they encode product design decisions as agent-accessible context in the repo using a product-design skill file. Agents understand why components exist, not just what shipped. Low-cost pattern applicable to any AI-assisted codebase.

- **Vibecoding gets Emacs patch rejected** — https://xlii.space/eng/honesty-gets-emacs-patch-rejected/
  A developer's AI-generated Emacs patch was rejected after they disclosed it was AI-written. Established open source communities are forming norms against AI-generated patches. Worth knowing before your next open source contribution.

- **An entire Herculaneum scroll has been read for the first time** — https://scrollprize.org/firstscroll
  Vesuvius Challenge succeeded: ML/vision models plus human expertise decoded an entire carbonized ancient scroll for the first time in ~2,000 years.

- **The papers-please era of the internet will decimate your privacy** — https://expression.fire.org/p/the-papers-please-era-of-the-internet
  Age verification laws requiring government ID for internet access are spreading globally. Regulatory risk for web product operators; could become mandatory compliance within 1-2 years in key markets.

- **The Exhaustion of Talking to a Tool** — https://ohadravid.github.io/posts/2026-06-tool-talking/
  Essay on the cognitive and emotional toll of heavy AI tool use. For builders using AI tools daily, a reflection on sustainable usage patterns.

## Full digest
- [P] [vercel-changelog] AI SDK 7 is now available — https://vercel.com/changelog/ai-sdk-7 — Major TypeScript AI SDK release with agent harnesses, file/skill uploads, MCP Apps; breaking changes
- [P] [hn-top] What happened after 2k people tried to hack my AI assistant — https://www.fernandoi.cl/posts/hackmyclaw/ — Real security learnings from exposing AI assistant to 2k attackers
- [P] [vercel-changelog] Teaching agents product design at Vercel — https://vercel.com/blog/teaching-agents-product-design-at-vercel — System for encoding product design decisions as agent-accessible repo context
- [P] [lobsters] Vibecoding gets Emacs patch rejected — https://xlii.space/eng/honesty-gets-emacs-patch-rejected/ — AI code disclosure triggered patch rejection; open source community norms forming
- [P] [hn-top] An entire Herculaneum scroll read for first time — https://scrollprize.org/firstscroll — ML/vision decoded full ancient scroll; Vesuvius Challenge milestone
- [P] [lobsters] The Exhaustion of Talking to a Tool — https://ohadravid.github.io/posts/2026-06-tool-talking/ — Essay on AI tool fatigue
- [M] [hn-top] The papers-please era of the internet — https://expression.fire.org/p/the-papers-please-era-of-the-internet — Age verification laws spreading; regulatory risk for web products
- [R] [google-ai-changelog] Interactions API — no content to assess
- [R] [vercel-changelog] AI SDK 7 blog post — duplicate of changelog entry
- [R] [vercel-changelog] Deep Agents and OpenCode harness — covered by AI SDK 7 announcement
- [R] [vercel-changelog] Pro teams 500 concurrent builds — large-team feature, not solo-dev relevant
- [R] [vercel-changelog] Preserve local env vars CLI — minor UX fix
- [R] [lobsters] font-family recommendations — reference, not news
- [R] [lobsters] Om Malik, 1966-2026 — obituary; not work-relevant; duplicate
- [R] [lobsters] New @bitCast Semantics Zig — niche Zig update
- [R] [lobsters] Oxide Rack 3D Explorer — hardware showcase
- [R] [hn-top] Om Malik has died — duplicate obituary
- [R] [hn-top] Libre Barcode Project — niche font utility
- [R] [hn-top] Framework 10G Ethernet module — hardware deep dive
- [R] [deferred x21] Various — deferred by pre-triage cap, not scored this run

results.title: Interactions API
results.summary: A new Interactions API page appeared in Google AI dev docs. No content was available to assess what changed.
results.raw_claim: Google added an Interactions API to the Gemini API documentation
results.uncertainty: No content fetched; cannot assess scope of changes
results.verdict_reason: No content available; cannot assess what changed

results.title: AI SDK 7
results.summary: Vercel published a blog post about AI SDK 7, covering the same content as the changelog entry. Preferring the changelog version.
results.raw_claim: Vercel published a blog post about AI SDK 7 covering new agent primitives, harnesses, and breaking changes
results.verdict_reason: Duplicate of changelog entry (2d80d9ec); same story, preferring changelog version

results.title: Teaching agents product design at Vercel
results.summary: Vercel describes their system for encoding product design decisions as agent-accessible context in the repo via a product-design skill directory. Agents understand why design patterns exist (not just what shipped). The SKILL.md routes tasks by mode (shape/implement/review/copy/harden) and points to canonical sources for component APIs, design-system rules, and interaction guidance. Low-cost pattern any AI-assisted builder can adapt.
results.raw_claim: Vercel built a product-design skill file system that gives coding agents access to product design rationale alongside code
results.worth_mentioning_reason: Directly applicable to any builder doing AI-assisted development; low-cost, high-leverage pattern
results.verdict_reason: Useful agent workflow pattern from a production team; worth reading for AI-assisted builders

results.title: AI SDK 7 is now available
results.summary: AI SDK 7 is a major release of the most-used TypeScript AI/agent SDK (16M weekly downloads). Breaking changes require migration via v7 codemods. Key additions: agent harness adapters for Claude Code, Codex, OpenCode, and Deep Agents behind a unified interface; standardized reasoning control across all providers; typed tool context for 3rd-party tools; file/skill upload APIs so large inputs are uploaded once and referenced by pointer; MCP Apps for server-rendered app UIs. Also expands realtime, video, transcription, and structured output. Read the migration guide before your next deploy.
results.raw_claim: Vercel released AI SDK 7 with breaking changes, unified agent harnesses, file/skill uploads, and MCP Apps support
results.uncertainty: Breaking changes not yet validated by 3rd-party builders in production
results.worth_mentioning_reason: Major version release with breaking changes for the most-downloaded TypeScript AI SDK
results.verdict_reason: Major release with breaking changes for the most-downloaded TypeScript AI SDK; agent harnesses + MCP Apps change how builders integrate AI

results.title: Deep Agents and OpenCode are now available in the AI SDK Harness
results.summary: Vercel added Deep Agents and OpenCode adapters to the AI SDK Harness, enabling these coding agent runtimes inside Vercel Sandboxes. Sub-announcement within AI SDK 7 release.
results.raw_claim: Deep Agents and OpenCode adapters are now available in the AI SDK Harness
results.verdict_reason: Covered by AI SDK 7 announcement; sub-feature of same release

results.title: Pro teams can now run up to 500 concurrent builds
results.summary: Vercel Pro teams can now run up to 500 concurrent builds (up from 12), billed by build minutes used.
results.raw_claim: Vercel increased concurrent build limits for Pro teams from 12 to 500
results.verdict_reason: Large-team feature; solo builders rarely hit concurrent build limits

results.title: Preserve local environment variables when linking with the Vercel CLI
results.summary: Vercel CLI now preserves .env.local when running vercel link instead of overwriting it.
results.raw_claim: Vercel CLI vercel link no longer overwrites existing .env.local file contents
results.verdict_reason: Minor UX fix; not decision-changing

results.title: The Exhaustion of Talking to a Tool
results.summary: Essay on the cognitive and emotional toll of constant interaction with AI coding tools. For solo builders using AI assistants daily, a reflection on sustainable usage patterns. Content not directly read; summary based on title and Lobsters discussion.
results.raw_claim: A developer wrote about the cognitive exhaustion of constantly interacting with AI coding tools in daily development work
results.uncertainty: Content not directly read; summary based on title and community discussion
results.worth_mentioning_reason: Qualitative signal for heavy AI tool users on sustainable usage
results.verdict_reason: Relevant reflection for heavy AI tool users; narrower but worth reading

results.title: Vibecoding gets Emacs patch rejected
results.summary: A developer submitted an AI-generated Emacs patch and disclosed AI involvement; the patch was rejected. The URL slug (honesty-gets-emacs-patch-rejected) suggests disclosure itself was the trigger. Open source communities are developing norms against AI-generated contributions. Worth knowing before submitting AI-assisted patches to established projects.
results.raw_claim: An AI-generated Emacs patch was rejected after the author disclosed it was AI-written
results.uncertainty: Content not directly read; summary based on title and URL slug
results.worth_mentioning_reason: Forming community norm around AI code disclosure in open source
results.verdict_reason: Signal about open source community norms around AI code; worth knowing for contributors

results.title: An entire Herculaneum scroll has been read for the first time
results.summary: The Vesuvius Challenge achieved its goal: ML/vision models and human expertise decoded an entire charred scroll from ancient Herculaneum for the first time in ~2,000 years. Preprint available at scrollprize.org/pdf/main.pdf. Significant milestone for AI applied to physical artifact restoration.
results.raw_claim: The Vesuvius Challenge successfully read an entire Herculaneum scroll using ML/vision models
results.worth_mentioning_reason: Significant ML milestone; demonstrates AI applied to physical artifact analysis
results.verdict_reason: Impressive ML milestone worth knowing; not directly actionable but historically significant

results.title: Framework's 10G Ethernet module exposes USB-C's complexity
results.summary: Jeff Geerling's analysis of USB-C protocol negotiation complexity with Framework's 10G Ethernet module.
results.raw_claim: Framework's 10G Ethernet module revealed deep complexity in USB-C protocol negotiation
results.verdict_reason: Hardware deep dive; not relevant to software builders

results.title: What happened after 2k people tried to hack my AI assistant
results.summary: Fernando Iazeolla built a public AI legal assistant (HackMyClaw) and invited 2,000+ people to try to break it. Documents real attack patterns, success rates, defense mechanisms, and lessons. First-hand empirical security research from a solo builder. If you ship any AI tool with user-controlled input, prompt injection and jailbreaking patterns from real attackers are directly applicable.
results.raw_claim: A builder exposed their AI legal assistant to 2,000+ people and documented real-world prompt injection and jailbreaking patterns
results.uncertainty: Content not directly read; summary based on title and community context
results.worth_mentioning_reason: Real security intelligence from a solo builder; actionable for anyone building AI tools with user input
results.verdict_reason: Direct empirical security research from a solo builder; actionable for anyone building AI tools with user input

results.title: The 'papers, please' era of the internet will decimate your privacy
results.summary: Analysis of how age verification laws requiring government ID are spreading globally (US, UK, EU and beyond). Creates compliance obligations, technical implementation challenges, and privacy risks for web products. No immediate action, but worth tracking for web product operators — requirements could materialize within 1-2 years in key markets.
results.raw_claim: Age verification laws requiring government ID for internet access are spreading globally, creating compliance and privacy risks for web products
results.uncertainty: Regulatory timeline and enforcement vary by jurisdiction
results.worth_mentioning_reason: Regulatory risk worth tracking for web product operators
results.verdict_reason: Regulatory risk in motion; not immediate but worth tracking for web product operators
